statement.p:
Identify the domain and range of each quadratic function.
w(x)=-3(x+10)^2-11
The domain is and the range is .
u(x)=4(x-7)^2+20
The domain is and the range is .
y(x)=-(x-1)^2
The domain is and the range is .
z(x)=3(x+9)^2-4
The domain is and the range is .

li.p: The domain of w is (-\infty,\infty)\text{.} The parabola opens downward so the range is (-\infty,-11]\text{.}
The domain of u is (-\infty,\infty)\text{.} The parabola opens upward so the range is [20,\infty)\text{.}
The domain of y is (-\infty,\infty)\text{.} The parabola opens downward so the range is (-\infty,0]\text{.}
The domain of z is (-\infty,\infty)\text{.} The parabola opens upward so the range is [-4,\infty)\text{.}

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.8.17) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context("Interval"); $domain = Interval("(-inf,inf)"); $range[0] = Interval("(-inf,-11]"); $range[1] = Interval("[20,inf)"); $range[2] = Interval("(-inf,0]"); $range[3] = Interval("[-4,inf)");
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Identify the domain and range of each quadratic function. a. [`w(x)=-3(x+10)^2-11`]
The domain is [_]{$domain}{5} and the range is [_]{$range[0]}{5}.
a. [`u(x)=4(x-7)^2+20`]
The domain is [_]{$domain}{5} and the range is [_]{$range[1]}{5}.
a. [`y(x)=-(x-1)^2`]
The domain is [_]{$domain}{5} and the range is [_]{$range[2]}{5}.
a. [`z(x)=3(x+9)^2-4`]
The domain is [_]{$domain}{5} and the range is [_]{$range[3]}{5}.
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION a. The domain of [`w`] is [`(-\infty,\infty)\text{.}`] The parabola opens downward so the range is [`(-\infty,-11]\text{.}`]
a. The domain of [`u`] is [`(-\infty,\infty)\text{.}`] The parabola opens upward so the range is [`[20,\infty)\text{.}`]
a. The domain of [`y`] is [`(-\infty,\infty)\text{.}`] The parabola opens downward so the range is [`(-\infty,0]\text{.}`]
a. The domain of [`z`] is [`(-\infty,\infty)\text{.}`] The parabola opens upward so the range is [`[-4,\infty)\text{.}`]
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
